surface.name: Rollbar SDK selection and first captured occurrence
surface.selection_basis: Under the workshop policy, the fastest commonly used documented route is selected instead of requiring a universal SDK choice. The official Browser JavaScript Quick Start is a copy-paste route with an explicit test command and dashboard-visible terminal, so it is primary; account signup is treated as one opaque step at the official signup URL.

alternatives_considered.name: Browser JavaScript quick start
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This provides a fully described browser-specific example but the general overview lists it alongside other language SDKs, and even the page recommends module imports for most modern web applications.

alternatives_considered.name: Node.js SDK
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a server-side JavaScript route requiring a different project token and code integration.

alternatives_considered.name: Rollbar REST API
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The general overview identifies SDK instrumentation as the first step rather than selecting a direct API route.

entry_point.developer_intent: Start without a Rollbar account or project, choose the SDK matching an application, instrument it with the correct project access token, send a documented test occurrence, and verify that the occurrence appears in Rollbar.
assumed_prior_state: No Rollbar account, project, SDK choice, access token, instrumented application, captured occurrence, or dashboard item is assumed.
A browser, working identity, application codebase, development environment, and network connection are available.
No signup method was authorized or submitted, account or project was created, token was viewed or generated, code was changed, or occurrence was sent during research.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The selected Rollbar SDK sends a test error or message from the instrumented application and the resulting item becomes visible in the Rollbar dashboard.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The simulated uncaught error appears in the project's Rollbar dashboard within a few seconds.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: This simulates an uncaught error. It should appear in your project's dashboard within a few seconds.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The dashboard-visible occurrence is the first proof that the browser SDK, project token, transport, and Rollbar ingestion path work end to end.

prerequisites.requirement: Create a Rollbar account using GitHub, Google, or email signup.
Create or select a Rollbar project for the application being instrumented.
Choose the SDK and integration route matching the application's language and framework.
Use the project access token with the scope required by the selected client-side or server-side SDK.
Have an application codebase and the ability to install or embed the selected SDK and run the application.

primary_path.action: Open the Browser JavaScript Quick Start.
details: This is the shortest official SDK route with a published test and terminal.
primary_path.output: The copy-paste snippet and test instructions are visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The Quick Start and Test your installation sections load.

primary_path.action: Sign up for a Rollbar account using the official signup URL.
details: Treat signup as one opaque step; do not infer provider-specific form fields.
primary_path.output: An authenticated Rollbar account is available.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer can access Rollbar and create a project.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Complete any account verification requested by Rollbar.

primary_path.action: Create or select a project and open Settings > Project Access Tokens.
details: Use a token with the `post_client_item` scope.
primary_path.output: A project and browser-capable project access token are available.
primary_path.success_signal: The token can be copied into the browser snippet.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: New tokens must be copied when created because they are not shown later.

primary_path.action: Paste the Quick Start snippet high in the page head before other scripts and replace `POST_CLIENT_ITEM_ACCESS_TOKEN`.
details: Change the environment value if the page is not production.
primary_path.input: The project token and an HTML page
primary_path.output: The page loads Rollbar.js with uncaught-error capture enabled.
primary_path.success_signal: The instrumented page loads in a browser.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Check snippet placement and token scope if the SDK does not initialize.

primary_path.action: Run the documented `window.onerror` test in the browser console.
details: On Firefox, use the documented `setTimeout(function() {notThere();}, 1000);` alternative in page code.
primary_path.output: A simulated uncaught error is sent to Rollbar.
primary_path.success_signal: The command runs and Rollbar receives the occurrence.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Use the Firefox-specific trigger when `window.onerror` is unavailable.

primary_path.action: Open the project dashboard and wait a few seconds.
primary_path.output: A Rollbar item for the test error is displayed.
primary_path.success_signal: The test occurrence appears in the project dashboard.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Recheck token, snippet placement, environment, and browser network errors.

branches.condition: The developer chooses an identity route
branches.path: Continue with GitHub, Google, or email registration.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the external authentication and verification sequence before project setup.

branches.condition: The application uses a listed language or framework
branches.path: Follow the corresponding JavaScript, Python, PHP, Ruby, Go, Apple, .NET, or framework SDK guide.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes installation, configuration, token scope, test action, and observable output.

branches.condition: The selected route is Browser JavaScript
branches.path: Use a post_client_item token, place the snippet high in the page head, load the page, and trigger the documented browser test error.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Provides a concrete dashboard-visible error terminal but is not universal.

branches.path: Use the published setTimeout undefined-function test instead of calling window.onerror directly.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes only the test-error trigger for the Browser JavaScript candidate.

friction_gates.description: A Rollbar account and project are required before obtaining a project token and seeing captured occurrences.
The public signup page does not expose the exact post-submission account and project states.
The developer must choose an SDK and often a framework-specific integration.
The integration requires a project token with an appropriate client or server scope.
Newly created access tokens are shown only at creation and cannot be retrieved from the UI or API afterward, so they must be copied and stored securely.
The SDK must be embedded or installed and configured in application code.
The Browser JavaScript quick start says the simulated error should appear in the dashboard in a few seconds.

sources.title: Rollbar Signup
sections_used: Get started with Rollbar for free

sources.title: Access Tokens
evidence_supported: One-time display for newly created tokens

excluded_after_success: Source maps, deploy tracking, person tracking, notifications, issue integrations, telemetry enrichment, additional environments, and production-quality error handling occur after the first captured occurrence is visible.
